message:
security(sanitize): Harden pattern matching and add verification

Security-critical improvements to the sanitization module:

## Pattern Matching Fixes

- FIXED: Password regex now preserves JSON structure by capturing the
  key and separator: (password|passwd|pwd)["\\s]*[:=]["\\s]*
- FIXED: Added pattern.lastIndex = 0 before each replace() call to
  ensure consistent behavior with global regex flags
- FIXED: ReDoS prevention in database URL pattern by:
  * Using non-greedy quantifiers
  * Limiting character sets to prevent backtracking explosion
  * Added redis:// protocol support

## Verification System

Enhanced verifySanitization() for audit compliance:

- Uses Set<string> for O(1) deduplication of warnings
- Non-mutating pattern checks (resets lastIndex before test)
- Returns structured { containsPotentialSecrets, warnings[] }
- Enables post-sanitization audit logging in diary generation

## Extra Patterns Handling

Improved normalizeExtraPatterns() for user-provided patterns:

- Accepts both RegExp and { pattern, replacement } formats
- Applies default replacement "[REDACTED_CUSTOM]" if not specified
- Preserves user-specified replacements when provided

## compileExtraPatterns()

- Added null-safety for patterns input
- Enhanced error messages with underlying error reason
- Returns valid RegExp[] for config-driven patterns

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-xhh","title":"[Cass] cassNeedsIndex - Check if index needs rebuild","description":"Implement cassNeedsIndex() to check if cass index needs rebuilding.

## Signature
```typescript
function cassNeedsIndex(cassPath = "cass"): boolean
```

## CLI Command
`cass health`

## Implementation
- Use execSync() to run cass health
- Check exit code to determine index health
- Return boolean indicating rebuild needed
- Suppress stdout/stderr with stdio: "pipe"

## Error Handling
- Return true if exit code 1 or 3 (needs rebuild)
- Return false if exit code 0 (healthy)
- Return false on any other error (assume healthy to avoid false positives)

## Exit Codes
- 0: Index is healthy, no rebuild needed
- 1: Index needs incremental update
- 3: Index missing or corrupted, needs full rebuild

## Usage
Used for proactive index maintenance:
```typescript
if (cassNeedsIndex()) {
  console.log("Rebuilding cass index...");
  await cassIndex({ full: true });
}
```","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:54.407263-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:47:37.937569-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-xiw","title":"[Validator] isGenericRule - Detect overly obvious rules","description":"Implement the isGenericRule function - Detect overly obvious rules that add no value.

SIGNATURE:
```typescript
function isGenericRule(content: string): boolean
```

GENERIC RULE EXAMPLES (REJECT THESE):
- "Write tests"
- "Use good variable names"
- "Handle errors"
- "Document your code"
- "Follow best practices"
- "Keep code DRY"
- "Use meaningful comments"

DETECTION HEURISTICS:

1. LENGTH CHECK:
   - Very short rules (< 20 chars) are likely generic
   - Example: "Write tests" (11 chars)

2. GENERIC TERM PATTERNS:
```typescript
const genericPatterns = [
  /^write tests?$/i,
  /^use (good|better|descriptive) (names|variables)/i,
  /^handle errors?$/i,
  /^document (your|the) code/i,
  /^follow best practices/i,
  /^keep.*DRY/i,
  /^avoid code duplication/i,
  /^use meaningful (names|comments)/i,
  /^always validate input/i,
  /^make code (readable|maintainable)/i
];
```

3. NO SPECIFIC TECHNOLOGY:
   - Generic: "Use dependency injection"
   - Specific: "Use React Context for dependency injection"
   - If no technology/framework/pattern mentioned → likely generic

4. NO ACTIONABLE DETAIL:
   - Generic: "Handle errors properly"
   - Specific: "Wrap async operations in try-catch and log to Sentry"

IMPLEMENTATION:
```typescript
function isGenericRule(content: string): boolean {
  // Check 1: Too short
  if (content.length < 20) {
    return true;
  }

  // Check 2: Generic patterns
  const genericPatterns = [
    /^write tests?$/i,
    /^use (good|better|descriptive) (names|variables)/i,
    /^handle errors?$/i,
    /^document (your|the) code/i,
    /^follow best practices/i,
    /^keep.*DRY/i,
    /^avoid code duplication/i,
    /^use meaningful (names|comments)/i,
    /^always validate input$/i,
    /^make code (readable|maintainable)/i
  ];

  if (genericPatterns.some(pattern => pattern.test(content))) {
    return true;
  }

  // Check 3: No specific technology mentioned
  const hasTechnology = /\b(React|TypeScript|Python|Node|PostgreSQL|Redis|Docker|AWS|API|REST|GraphQL|SQL)\b/i.test(content);
  const hasPattern = /\b(dependency injection|event sourcing|CQRS|microservices|singleton|factory|strategy)\b/i.test(content);

  if (!hasTechnology && !hasPattern && content.length < 50) {
    return true;  // Short, generic, no specifics
  }

  return false;
}
```

EXAMPLES:

Generic (return true):
- "Write unit tests"
- "Use descriptive variable names"
- "Handle errors properly"
- "Keep functions small"
- "Avoid code duplication"

Specific (return false):
- "Use TypeScript strict mode with noImplicitAny enabled"
- "Wrap all Prisma queries in try-catch blocks and log errors to Sentry"
- "Use React.memo for expensive component renders in data tables"
- "Implement retry logic with exponential backoff for external API calls"

WHY THIS MATTERS:
- Prevents playbook pollution with obvious advice
- Focuses validation resources on meaningful rules
- Improves playbook quality and signal-to-noise ratio
- Saves LLM costs by rejecting generics early

DEPENDENCIES:
- None (pure function)

- src/validator/generic-rules.ts","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:59.729673-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:14:51.429108-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-xjk","title":"[Utils] scoreBulletRelevance - Keyword-based bullet scoring","description":"","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:23.661135-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:23.661135-05:00","comments":[{"id":35,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-xjk","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Function Signature
```typescript
function scoreBulletRelevance(
  bulletContent: string,
  bulletTags: string[],
  keywords: string[]
): number
```

## Implementation Details
- Initialize score = 0
- For each keyword in keywords:
  - If bulletContent.toLowerCase() includes keyword: score += 2
  - If bulletTags includes keyword (case-insensitive): score += 3
- Return total score
- Higher score = more relevant to task

## Why This Matters
Used in context command to rank bullets by task relevance. Bullets with matching keywords are more likely to be useful for current task. Tag matches weighted higher (3 vs 2) because tags are more intentional than content mentions.

## Edge Cases to Handle
- Empty keywords - return 0
- Empty bulletContent and bulletTags - return 0
- Case sensitivity - always compare lowercase
- Partial matches - "test" matches "testing" (use includes)
- Multiple keyword matches - accumulate score
- Same keyword in content and tags - count both (score += 5 total)","created_at":"2025-12-07T23:15:50Z"}]}
-{"id":"cass_memory_system-xjx","title":"[Reflector] deduplicateDeltas - Remove duplicate insights","description":"function deduplicateDeltas(
  newDeltas: PlaybookDelta[],
  seenHashes: Set<string>
): PlaybookDelta[]

For each delta:
- Hash the delta (content + type)
- If hash in seenHashes, skip
- Otherwise add hash and keep delta
- Return unique deltas

PURPOSE:
Prevents same insight being proposed multiple times across iterations

EXAMPLE SCENARIO:
- Iteration 0: LLM proposes "Use async/await for promises"
- Iteration 1: LLM proposes same insight with slightly different wording
- Deduplication: Hash matches, second proposal is skipped

ALGORITHM:
1. Create result array
2. For each delta in newDeltas:
   - hash = hashDelta(delta)
   - if (seenHashes.has(hash)) continue
   - seenHashes.add(hash)
   - result.push(delta)
3. Return result

DEPENDENCIES:
- hashDelta()

Used in reflectOnSession() main loop","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:54.645079-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:48:52.840638-05:00"}

  newDeltas: PlaybookDelta[],
  seenHashes: Set<string>
): PlaybookDelta[]

For each delta:
- Hash the delta (content + type)
- If hash in seenHashes, skip
- Otherwise add hash and keep delta
- Return unique deltas

PURPOSE:
Prevents same insight being proposed multiple times across iterations

EXAMPLE SCENARIO:
- Iteration 0: LLM proposes "Use async/await for promises"
- Iteration 1: LLM proposes same insight with slightly different wording
- Deduplication: Hash matches, second proposal is skipped

ALGORITHM:
1. Create result array
2. For each delta in newDeltas:
   - hash = hashDelta(delta)
   - if (seenHashes.has(hash)) continue
   - seenHashes.add(hash)
   - result.push(delta)
3. Return result

DEPENDENCIES:
- hashDelta()

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-xtj","title":"[Utils] expandPath - Home directory expansion","description":"","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:45.074017-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:48:03.209209-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T18:47:47.159171-05:00","comments":[{"id":36,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-xtj","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Function Signature
```typescript
function expandPath(p: string): string
```

## Implementation Details
- Check if path starts with "~/"
- If yes, replace with os.homedir() + path.slice(1)
- Otherwise return path unchanged
- Use Node.js os.homedir() for cross-platform compatibility

## Why This Matters
Critical for config paths like "~/.cass-memory/playbook.yaml". Users expect tilde expansion to work like in shell commands. Without this, paths starting with ~ would fail to resolve.

## Edge Cases to Handle
- Empty string input - return unchanged
- Path with only "~" (no slash) - should expand to home directory
- Paths like "~user/file" (other user's home) - NOT supported, return unchanged
- Windows paths (C:\\Users\\...) - return unchanged, no expansion needed
- Null/undefined input - should throw or return empty string","created_at":"2025-12-07T23:15:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-xw0","title":"[Utils] generateDiaryId - Unique diary entry identifier","description":"","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:00.048552-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:00.048552-05:00","comments":[{"id":37,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-xw0","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Function Signature
```typescript
function generateDiaryId(sessionPath: string): string
```

## Implementation Details
- Format: "diary-{hash}"
- Combine sessionPath + Date.now() into single string
- Hash using hashContent() (which uses SHA-256)
- Prefix with "diary-" for clarity
- Deterministic for same session at same millisecond

## Why This Matters
Diary entries need unique IDs that are tied to session but not globally unique. This allows same session to have multiple diary entries if processed at different times, while preventing exact duplicates.

## Edge Cases to Handle
- Same session processed twice in same ms - same ID (prevents duplicate diary entries)
- Same session processed at different times - different IDs
- Different sessions - different IDs
- Empty sessionPath - still generates ID based on timestamp","created_at":"2025-12-07T23:15:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-y22j","title":"[Docs] Configuration reference documentation","description":"Document all configuration options with types, defaults, and examples.

Configuration sources (precedence order):
1. Command-line flags (highest)
2. Environment variables
3. Repo config (.cass/config.yaml)
4. Global config (~/.cass-memory/config.json)
5. Defaults (lowest)

Global config: llm_provider, default_model, confidence_threshold, decay_half_life_days, max_bullets_per_section
Repo config: project_name, playbook_path, diary_path, session_path, validation_rules
Environment variables: OPENAI_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, CASS_MEMORY_CONFIG, CASS_MEMORY_DEBUG

Include JSON schema for validation and example configs","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:49:28.411185-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:51:13.944052-05:00"}
